points.speaker: Annette Lin
points.text: Annette Lin announced the meeting was conducted under California Government Code 54953(e) with no physical location; all commissioners participated by videoconference, and public comments were accepted via Zoom. Emails received by 5:00 PM were forwarded to the Commission.
points.source: Annette Lin : All members of the HRC will participate in the meeting by video conference with no physical meeting location.

points.speaker: Harsha Ramchandani
points.text: Staff proposed principles to align expenditures with Consolidated Plan goals, administer capital funds flexibly (e.g., direct allocations such as the Crestview conversion or competitive NOFAs), and establish minimum funding thresholds due to federal reporting and monitoring burdens for recipients and the City.
points.source: Harsha Ramchandani : The first principle is that all expenditures should fund activities that further the goals of the City's consolidated plan... staff recommends analyzing the best use and method of allocating the capital funds on an annual basis.

points.speaker: Harsha Ramchandani
points.text: For public services, staff recommended a $7,500 minimum request for CDBG grants (with a $5,000 hard floor), and applying the same threshold to General Fund public service grants under Council Policy A-8, balancing administrative burden and broad demand for limited funds.
points.source: Harsha Ramchandani : The hard minimum is $5000. Staff commends a grant request of 7500 for the CDBG Public Services grants and per Council policy A8. This threshold would also apply for the General Fund Public Service Grants.

points.speaker: Michaela Helman-Tincher
points.text: On the $30,000 minor home repair set-aside, staff said the amount reflects nonprofit demand estimates and capacity; they plan to reintroduce and publicize the program, monitor uptake, and adjust funding in future years if demand proves higher to avoid unspent funds penalties.
points.source: Michaela Helman-Tincher : ...we did reach out to some nonprofits... that's how we came up with the 30,000 recommendation... bring back the program, get the word out and if we see that the demand is high, we can adjust it next year as well.

points.speaker: Michaela Helman-Tincher
points.text: Staff noted many recommendations formalize existing practice while preserving discretion. Exceptions (e.g., capital requests below $300,000) may be considered case by case, and NOFAs can state HRC/Council discretion alongside published evaluation criteria to be summarized for HRC in March.
points.source: Michaela Helman-Tincher : ...at the discretion of the HRC and and City Council, you know there, there may be exceptions.

points.speaker: Michaela Helman-Tincher
points.text: City has four affordable housing projects currently approved by Council to move forward, with two more likely in next year.
points.source: Michaela Helman-Tincher : We have, I believe, have to count think 4 projects that are currently approved by the council to move forward

points.speaker: Michaela Helman-Tincher
points.text: City anticipates funding gap for affordable housing pipeline and Council recently approved funding for Terra Bella housing development.
points.source: Michaela Helman-Tincher : we do anticipate A funding gap for our pipeline at large

points.speaker: Annette Lin
points.text: The Mental Health project subcommittee has not met and had no updates to report at this meeting.
points.source: Annette Lin : ...we do have another subcommittee from the mental health project, but they haven't. My understanding is they haven't met yet.

points.speaker: Julie Solomon
points.text: Three community members are interested in being interviewed about housing instability experiences, with contact information provided to staff.
points.source: Julie Solomon : there are three members of the community who would be interested potentially to be interviewed together and share their stories together about housing instability

points.speaker: Laurel James
points.text: Library events include an online talk with Kwame Christian on November 15 at 9:00 AM (registration required) and an in-person film screening on James Baldwin's work on November 19 at 3:00 PM (no advance registration).
points.source: Laurel James : ...author conversation with Kwame Christian... Tuesday, November 15th at 9:00 AM... and the 2nd is a film screening about James Baldwin's work on Saturday, November 19th at 3:00 PM.

points.speaker: Annette Lin
points.text: Annette Lin thanked housing staff for their presentation and Q&A. Commissioners expressed appreciation for Annette Lin's service and wished her well as she relocates. The meeting adjourned, with the next regular HRC meeting scheduled for December 1, 2022 at 6:00 PM.
points.source: Annette Lin : I'd like to thank staff for for giving this great presentation... I will go ahead and adjourn to the next regular meeting... December 1st, 2022 at six.

points.speaker: Commissioners
points.text: Commissioners thanked Chair Lin for her service and contributions to the city, wishing her well in her move to San Mateo.
points.source: Jill Rakestraw : thank you for everything you've done for the city and I hope that you enjoy your new home outside Mountain View

decisions.text: Subcommittee will meet tomorrow to discuss existing community resources and program explanations.
decisions.source: Jill Rakestraw : We have our next meeting tomorrow

decisions.text: Subcommittee will reconvene in couple weeks to develop framework for housing security project.
decisions.source: Xavier Webb : We're going to get back together in a couple more weeks to try and keep keep hashing it out to to try and get something, some kind of framework around it

gt1_point.text: Housing staff will issue the CDBG and General Fund NOFA in early December 2022 and close it by January 2023.
gt1_point.source: Harsha Ramchandani : ...issuing a notice of CDBG and general fund funding availability in early December 2022. The NOFA will close by January of 2023.

gt2_point.text: Staff will issue CDBG and General Fund NOFA in early December 2022, with applications closing in January 2023.
gt2_point.source: Harsha Ramchandani : The timeline in line for the next steps includes issuing a notice of CDBG and general fund funding availability in early December 2022

needreview_gt.question: Should the assignee be 'Housing staff' (GT1) or 'Staff' (GT2) for issuing the CDBG and General Fund NOFA?
needreview_gt.merge_if_valid: Use the more specific assignee designation that is validated by transcript

gt1_point.text: Michaela Helman-Tincher will research whether CDBG/HOME funds can support earthquake retrofits and report back.
gt1_point.source: Michaela Helman-Tincher : The short answer is I'm not quite sure we will do some research.

gt2_point.text: Staff will bring funding recommendations back to HRC for consideration in March 2023.
gt2_point.source: Harsha Ramchandani : Public service and capital funding applications and staff recommendations will be brought back to the HRC for consideration in March 2023

needreview_gt.question: Are these two different decisions, or is the GT2 decision about bringing recommendations back in March a separate action from GT1's earthquake retrofit research task?
needreview_gt.merge_if_valid: Keep as separate decisions if they refer to different actions

gt1_point.text: City Council will conduct interviews for HRC vacancies on November 21.
gt1_point.source: Laurel James : ...interviews will be conducted on November 21st with the City Council.

needreview_gt.question: GT1 has a decision assigned to 'City Council' for conducting interviews on November 21, but GT2 only mentions this as a point under Laurel James. Should this be treated as a decision with City Council as assignee?
needreview_gt.merge_if_valid: Include as decision with City Council assignee if transcript confirms this as an action item

gt1_point.text: Staff highlighted a robust affordable housing pipeline and anticipated funding gaps; projects include Crestview hotel opening next year, La Avenida starting construction, Montecito and Lot 12 to follow, and Terra Bella funded. HOME funds would be allocated across pipeline projects to meet HUD timelines.
gt1_point.source: Michaela Helman-Tincher : ...we do anticipate the Crestview hotel opening next year, the LA Avenida affordable housing development starting construction next year... Montecito... LOT 12... funding for the Terra Bella housing development...
gt1_point.speaker: Michaela Helman-Tincher

gt2_point.text: Crestview hotel conversion expected to open next year, with LA Avenida development starting construction and other projects following.
gt2_point.source: Michaela Helman-Tincher : we do anticipate the Crestview hotel opening next year, the LA Avenida affordable housing development starting construction next year
gt2_point.speaker: Michaela Helman-Tincher

needreview_gt.question: GT1 provides more detailed project names (Montecito, Lot 12, Terra Bella) while GT2 is more general. Should these be merged with GT1's more detailed version, or are they describing different aspects of the pipeline discussion?
needreview_gt.merge_if_valid: Merge using GT1's more comprehensive version if transcript confirms all project names were mentioned

gt1_point.text: Addressing a question on earthquake retrofitting, Michaela Helman-Tincher said she will research whether federal funds could support such work given restrictions and scale. Wayne Chen added a soft-story retrofit evaluation began about three years ago but was paused due to costs, staff capacity, and the pandemic.
gt1_point.source: Michaela Helman-Tincher : The short answer is I'm not quite sure we will do some research.
gt1_point.speaker: Michaela Helman-Tincher

needreview_gt.question: GT1 has a detailed point about earthquake retrofitting discussion including both Michaela's research commitment and Wayne's historical context, but GT2 only has Wayne's historical context separately. Should this combined point be included or split?
needreview_gt.merge_if_valid: Include if transcript confirms this was a single discussion combining both speakers' contributions

gt1_point.text: The Housing and Security Subcommittee met (members: Lin, Vice Chair Sylvester, Webb) with input from community member Malia Perez (Reach Potential). They are exploring both events and an online storytelling artifact (e.g., Humans of New York style) to amplify residents' housing experiences; plans remain exploratory.
gt1_point.source: Annette Lin : ...we were also able to have... Malia Perez, join us... the idea of... Humans of New York where they tell the stories of local residents... letting their stories be told... and being able to amplify their voices.
gt1_point.speaker: Annette Lin

gt2_point.text: Housing and Security Subcommittee met this week with Commissioner Webb, Vice Chair Sylvester, and community member Malia Perez to discuss potential projects.
gt2_point.source: Annette Lin : So we were able to meet earlier this week
gt2_point.speaker: Annette Lin

needreview_gt.question: GT1 provides specific details about the 'Humans of New York' style storytelling project while GT2 is more general about 'potential projects'. Should these be merged with GT1's more detailed version?
needreview_gt.merge_if_valid: Merge using GT1's more detailed version if transcript confirms the storytelling project discussion

merge_stats.notes: Good consensus on core meeting content and funding details. Main uncertainties involve assignee specificity (Housing staff vs Staff), project detail levels, and whether some combined points should be split. Most conflicts are about level of detail rather than factual disagreements.
